【now.com體育】聖誕節臨近，利物浦為應節就安排球員進行一場忍笑比賽，以你問我答的形式找出誰是紅軍忍笑王。

今次比賽參賽者分別是羅拔圖法明奴（Roberto Firmino）、菲臘比古天奴（Philippe Coutinho）、祖爾麥迪比及史杜列治，他們需要說一些有關聖誕節的笑話，如果能惹對手發笑就獲得1分。 法明奴及古天奴率先在充滿聖誕節擺設的房間中對壘，由法明奴先進攻：「你知道為甚麼聖誕老人不去愛華頓嗎？因為他總是穿著紅色衣服。」古天奴已經笑個不停，之後法明奴憑「聖誕老人喜歡的紅軍球員是古天奴（Philippe Coutinho-ho-ho）」的諧音笑話再下一城，結果輕鬆大勝4:1。 祖爾麥迪比及史杜列治的比賽就激烈得多，麥迪比尚未開始說笑話，史杜列治已經失守，雖然史杜列治一度以「聖誕老人不去愛華頓」的笑話追平比分，但最終祖爾麥迪比以「紙片男被吹走的故事」擊敗史杜列治，險勝2:1。 「聖誕老人不去愛華頓」的笑話似乎非常受紅軍球員歡迎，不過你不找人、人亦找你，愛華頓周日將造訪晏菲路球場，上演默西塞德打吡。 相關片段 http://dailym.ai/2kEXmE4

Liverpool CEO Peter Moore has said the club will "continue to look at acquisitions that will strengthen the squad" ahead of the January transfer window.

Moore also played down speculation that Philippe Coutinho could leave for Barcelona in the winter, telling Sky Sports: "As far as I'm concerned, the squad we have right now is the squad we will go into January with."

Liverpool did not add to their squad last January and also failed to achieve a long-mooted summer move for Southampton defender Virgil van Dijk.

But Moore suggested manager Jurgen Klopp could make additions this time and dismissed criticism that Liverpool had not spent enough in recent windows.

"The chemistry we bring on to the field isn't about buying the most expensive players," he said.

"We haven't been shy -- people look at the Mohamed Salah acquisition as the best one of the summer and we're already bringing in Naby Keita [from RB Leipzig] next summer.

"That money has been spent, Alex Oxlade-Chamberlain has also been brought in.

"The investments are being made and it's hard to knock what you're seeing on the field now and say: 'Oh, you should be spending more money.'

"What I will say, because I know you are leading up to the question about January, the club continues to look at acquisitions that will strengthen the squad."

Moore said Liverpool owners Fenway Sports Group had offered good backing to managers in the transfer market since taking over at Anfield in 2010.

"When I look back at what this squad looked like back in 2010 when FSG acquired it and where we are today, particularly the bench, I think the squad is in great shape," he said.

And addressing continued speculation about the departure of Brazil playmaker Coutinho, Moore said: "We made one statement last year that the player is not for sale and that's the statement the ownership made.

"Every day, you see stories emanating from Spain or wherever that Barcelona or whomever are coming back for Philippe Coutinho.

"I saw a player that was delighted to score a hat trick for his club on Wednesday night [in the 7-0 Champions League demolition of Spartak Moscow].

"The focus right now is on Sunday, when we've got West Brom, and it's all about Philippe on the pitch right now. Nothing I can say right now is going to add to that.

"As far as I'm concerned, the squad we have right now is the squad we will go into January with.

"I don't have a crystal ball, but Philippe Coutinho is a dedicated, intense Liverpool player right now, who is delivering."
